input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade, the .testsettings file seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Timeout for each test method (in milliseconds) -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment config
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The configuration needs to be migrated to a `.runsettings` file and/or MSTest v3 attributes/properties.

Let me explain the migration:

1. **Test timeout**: In MSTest v3, you can set the default timeout via `[Timeout]` attribute or via `.runsettings` under `<MSTest><TestTimeout>`.


2. **Code coverage data collector**: This goes into a `.runsettings` file under `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>`.
3. **Assembly resolution / deployment**: MSTest v3 handles deployment differently. The `useLoadContext` setting is no longer needed. Deployment can be configured in `.runsettings` under `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>`.

Now I'll create a `.runsettings` file that replaces the `.testsettings` configuration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Timeout for each test method (in milliseconds) -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
MSTest v3 **dropped support for `.testsettings` files**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file instead. I've created `local.runsettings` that maps your three settings:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` (30s) | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` |
| Code Coverage data collector | `<DataCollectors>` section (updated to v2.0 URI) |

**To use it**, either:
- Run tests with: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or wire it into your `.csproj`:
  ```xml
  <PropertyGroup>
    <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  ```

You can then safely delete `local.testsettings`.
